items.text: Family history: asthma, allergy, COPD in family
Duration and nature of symptoms: dyspnea, wheezing, chest tightness, cough
Timing: at rest / during exercise / post-exercise / at night
Triggers: cold, dust, animals, odors, exercise, infections
Allergies: pollen, animal, other — physician-confirmed?
Smoking / snus / e-cigarettes
Work environment: dust, gases, fumes
Comorbidities: reflux, sleep apnea, depression, arrhythmia
Medications: current asthma drugs (maintenance + PRN), name them
Asthma control: weekly symptoms, night symptoms, reliever use, functional capacity
Spirometry + bronchodilator test (FEV₁/FVC, ≥12% and 200 mL improvement)
PEF monitoring 2 weeks (≥20% and 60 L/min diurnal variation OR ≥15% bronchodilator response)
Consider bronchial provocation: methacholine (PD₂₀FEV₁ ≤0.6 mg), mannitol, exercise test
FeNO (inflammatory marker)
Allergy screening / prick tests
Chest X-ray (if indicated)
Blood tests: CBC, CRP, total IgE, specific IgE (if indicated)

tables.caption: Treatment Steps
rows: SABA as needed OR ICS-formoterol as needed

items.text: Symptoms ≤2×/week → well controlled
No night symptoms
No activity limitation
Reliever ≤2×/week
Well controlled 6–12 months → consider step-down
Exercise symptoms: cough, dyspnea, wheezing during or after exercise
Cold air exposure: symptoms when training in cold
Training volume: hours/week, sports, endurance/strength split %
Respiratory infections and sick days last season
EILO screening: laryngeal symptoms at rest/exercise/after (Newcastle questionnaire)
Breathing devices: WellO₂, Sarvikuono, Airtrim, Lungplus
Consider EVH test or mannitol test (eucapnic voluntary hyperventilation)
Exercise challenge: treadmill or field test with symptom assessment
Reflux symptoms: burning, belching, acid taste (common in athlete cough)
Return-to-sport: symptom-free, medication optimized, PEF stable
Prednisolone 20–40 mg/day for 5–7 days
Increase reliever medication
Check inhaler technique
Assess adherence
Follow-up visit in 2–4 weeks
Inhaler technique taught and checked
Self-monitoring instructions (PEF, symptom diary)
Exacerbation action plan (when to seek care)
Medication reimbursement (Kela B-statement for asthma drugs)
Follow-up appointment booked
